figures.caption: Figure 1. Schematic diagram of the D3V91(I) discrete velocity set.
Figure 2. Decomposition of the D3V91(I) discrete velocity set.
Figure 3. Schematic diagram of the D3V91(II) discrete velocity set.
Figure 4. Decomposition of the D3V91(II) discrete velocity set.
Figure 5. Comparison between the DBM simulation and the Riemann solution for the Sod shock tube at t = 0.1: (a) density, (b) pressure, (c) velocity and (d) temperature.
Figure 6. Comparison between the DBM simulation and the Riemann solution for the Lax shock tube at t = 0.1: (a) density, (b) pressure, (c) velocity and (d) temperature.

Figure 7. Distributions of the TNE quantities computed using the 2nd NND scheme (left column) and the 5th WENO scheme (right column). Blue open circles denote the DBM results. The green dash-dotted, black dashed and red solid lines denote the first-, second- and third-order analytical solutions, respectively.

Figure 8. Distributions of ∆ ∗ 3xxx for the weak (I), moderate (II), and strong (III) cases computed using the D3V55 model (left column) and the D3V91 model (right column). Blue open circles denote the DBM results. The green dash-dotted and red solid lines denote the firstand second-order analytical solutions, respectively. The corresponding times for cases I–III are t=0.012, 0.012, and 0.014, respectively.

Figure 9. Effects of the velocity gradient (a) and density gradient (b) on the nonequilibrium quantity ∆ ∗ 3: linear and exponential responses, where for (a): A1 = 0.051, B1 = 0.010, A2 = 0.023, B2 = 1.102, C2 = 0.003; for (b): A1 = 0.011, B1 = 0.713, C1 = 0.016, A2 = 0.001, B2 = 0.032.

Figure 10. Distributions of ∆ ∗ 4,2xx for the weak (I), moderate (II) and strong (III) cases computed using the D3V55 model (left column) and the D3V91 model (right column). Blue open circles denote the DBM results. The green dash-dotted and red solid lines denote the firstand second-order analytical solutions, respectively. The corresponding times for cases I–III are t = 0.002, 0.008 and 0.0055, respectively.

Figure 11. Effects of the density gradient (a) and temperature gradient (b) on the nonequilibrium quantity ∆ ∗ 4,2: linear and exponential responses, where for (a): A1 = 0.025, B1 = 2.185, C1 = 0.001, A2 = 0.090, B2 = 0.066; for (b): A1 = 0.124, B1 = −0.192, A2 = 0.141, B2 = 0.404, C2 = −0.275.

Figure 12. Distributions of∆∗ 2xx for the weak (I), moderate (II) and strong (III) cases computed using the D3V55 model (left column) and the D3V91 model (right column). Blue open circles denote the DBM results. The green dash-dotted, black dashed and red solid lines denote the first-, second- and third-order analytical solutions, respectively. The corresponding times for cases I–III are t = 0.015, 0.0325 and 0.03, respectively.
Table 1: Relative TNE intensities of the viscous stress for the weak (I), moderate (II) and strong (III) nonequilibrium cases.

Figure 13. Effects of the density gradient (a) and temperature gradient (b) on the nonequilibrium quantity ∆ ∗ 2: linear and exponential responses, where for (a): A1 = 0.095, B1 = 0.153, C1 = −0.089, A2 = 0.005, B2 = 0.026; for (b): A1 = 0.051, B1 = −0.066, A2 = 0.290, B2 = 0.139, C2 = −0.352.

Figure 14. Distributions of ∆ ∗ 3,1x for the weak (I), moderate (II), and strong (III) cases computed using the D3V55 model (left column) and the D3V91 model (right column). Blue open circles denote the DBM results. The green dash-dotted, black dashed and red solid lines denote the first-, second- and third-order analytical solutions, respectively. The corresponding times for cases I–III are t = 0.015, 0.018, and 0.016, respectively.
Table 2: Relative nonequilibrium intensities of the heat flux for weak (I), moderate (II), and strong (III) nonequilibrium conditions.

Figure 15. Effects of the density gradient (a) and transition-layer width (b) on the heat flux ∆ ∗ 3,1: exponential and linear responses, where for (a): A1 = 0.013, B1 = 0.917, C1 = −0.005, A2 = 0.006, B2 = 0.002; for (b): A1 = −0.703, B1 = −0.110, C1 = 0.012, A2 = −0.002, B2 = 0.061.
Table 3: Discrete velocity set D3V91(I), with 91 discrete velocities.
Table 4: Discrete velocity set D3V91(II), with 91 discrete velocities.
Table 5: Expressions for ∆ ∗(1) 3 and ∆ ∗(1) 4,2 .
Table 6: Expressions for ∆ ∗(2) 3 and ∆ ∗(2) 4,2 .
